mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, causing short-term equity sell-offs as investors price in lower near-term output and earnings.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 70% of periods following a 0.5% drop in INDPRO, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL breaching 240.0 indicates persistent price pressures that force central banks to accelerate tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate signals labor market inefficiency and potential economic deceleration, leading to negative equity returns as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in more than 58% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly translates to higher corporate profit margins and revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation (p > 0.10) with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production during periods of tight monetary policy indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, supporting positive short-term equity returns as earnings beat expectations.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 5-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term predictive accuracy drops below random chance (hit rate < 50%) during tightening phases.
